human_intro: Supabase Auth vs Firebase Auth is the classic platform decision. Both offer 50K MAU free. Both tie you to an ecosystem. I compared them on Next.js App Router compatibility, RLS vs Firestore rules, and which one hurts less to migrate away from.

seo.meta_title: Supabase Auth vs Firebase Auth for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins? (Updated Week 24, 2026)
seo.meta_description: Supabase Auth (4.4★) vs Firebase Auth (4.3★) for Next.js. Compare Easy vs Easy learning curves, free tiers, and get our recommendation.
seo.og_title: Supabase Auth vs Firebase Auth — Which Next.js Auth Tool Wins?
seo.og_description: Supabase Auth (4.4★, Easy) and Firebase Auth (4.3★, Easy) compared for Next.js. Who wins on setup, pricing, and DX?

hero.headline: Supabase Auth vs Firebase Auth for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Supabase Auth and Firebase Auth — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Putting Supabase Auth and Firebase Auth through the same Next.js project reveals a clear pattern: Supabase Auth handles server components, middleware, and edge runtime better. The 7/10 score reflects real-world testing. Firebase Auth is still a capable option, especially if you care about Free – Pay-as-you-go.
verdict_text: Supabase Auth is the safer bet for Next.js projects. Its 15 minutes setup time and easy learning curve mean you're productive faster. Firebase Auth isn't far behind  –  especially if you need yes, up to 50,000 mau free tier  –  but Supabase Auth has the edge where it counts for App Router development.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t1: Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier
structured_data_points.t2: Yes, up to 50,000 MAU free tier

pros: Extremely generous free tier with 50,000 monthly active users
Integrated with Supabase database, storage, and realtime subscriptions
Built-in support for email, phone, OAuth, and magic links
cons: Tightly coupled to the Supabase PostgreSQL ecosystem
Less focused on auth-only use cases - it's part of a larger platform
Organization and team management features are less mature than Clerk
RLS policies can become complex to manage at scale

pros: Massive free tier with 50,000 MAU at no cost
Tightly integrated with Firebase and Google Cloud ecosystem
Supports email/password, phone, Google, Apple, and 10+ social providers
Well-documented with abundant tutorials and community resources
Anonymous auth for gradual user onboarding
cons: Google ecosystem lock-in - less portable than open-source alternatives
No built-in Next.js App Router server component helpers
Client-side heavy - requires careful setup for SSR
Google deprecation history creates platform risk
Limited organization and role management features

faq.question: Should I choose Supabase Auth or Firebase Auth for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Supabase Auth scores 7/10 for Next.js integration with a Easy learning curve. Firebase Auth scores 6/10 with a Easy learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Supabase Auth because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier or Yes, up to 50,000 MAU free tier is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Supabase Auth or Firebase Auth more affordable?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth starts at Free – $75/mo with a free tier of Yes, 50,000 MAU included in Free tier. Firebase Auth starts at Free – Pay-as-you-go with a free tier of Yes, up to 50,000 MAU free tier. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Firebase Auth offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Supabase Auth or Firebase Auth easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. Firebase Auth has a Easy learning curve with an estimated setup time of 15 minutes. Firebase Auth is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Supabase Auth and Firebase Auth together?
faq.answer: Using both Supabase Auth and Firebase Auth in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Supabase Auth or Firebase Auth?
faq.answer: Supabase Auth has a Easy learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 7/10, and a user rating of 4.4★. Firebase Auth has a Easy learning curve, 6/10, and 4.3★. On paper, Supabase Auth leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
